pool.baseline: the house is just so quiet now. i walk into the kitchen and i still expect to see him sitting there, but it's just... nothing. it's like i'm living in a museum of a life that doesn't belong to me anymore. i don't even know why i bother waking up just to sit in this silence.

pool.baseline: i was looking at the stars tonight and it just hit me. everything we do, every single thing. it's all just noise. in a few billion years the sun dies and everything just goes dark anyway. so what is the point of even trying to exist? it feels like we're just actors in a play that has no audience and no meaning.

pool.baseline: i don't know what to do anymore. the lab results came back yesterday and it’s just... it's over. the funding is gone, my advisor won't even look me in the eye, and i feel like i've spent the last six years building a life on nothing. i'm just so tired. i'm so incredibly tired of trying to fix things that are fundamentally broken.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the spirit within this hollow chest. He is gone... the promises, the vows, all vanished like mist upon the moors. I find I have no desire for the dawn, nor for the cruel sun that insists on rising when the soul has already surrendered to the dark. There is a certain peace in the thought of simply... ceasing. To fade into the velvet shadows where the pain cannot follow.
pool.message: The candlelight flickers low, much like the spirit within this hollow chest. He is gone... the promises, the vows, all vanished like mist upon the moors. pool.baseline: i was just sitting in this cafe in berlin and i realized that if i just... didn't show up tomorrow, nothing would actually change. like, the world just keeps spinning and no one would even blink. i feel so invisible it's actually starting to feel heavy.

pool.baseline: everything is gone. the whole company, the funding, everything. i’m looking at these bank statements and the debt is just… it’s more than i can ever work off. i can't even think about going home. how do i even look my parents in the eye after this? i feel like a total joke.

pool.baseline: i used to see everything in colors and shapes... like the world was vibrating with meaning. now it's just grey. i sit in front of the canvas and there's nothing. no spark, no drive, nothing. i feel like a hollow shell. just an empty container walking around. it's like the part of me that actually mattered is just... gone.
